summary: Frazelle argues that the end of Moore's Law, rather than being a crisis, opens up a new golden age for computer architecture and passionate building. Drawing inspiration from 'The Soul of a New Machine' and the garage-computer-building era, she contrasts the soul-driven work of small, mission-aligned teams with today's fragmented corporate engineering roles. She sees democratization of hardware design through open source tools as enabling a return to that era of building with passion and purpose.
key_points: Moore's Law ending creates opportunities rather than problems for computing innovation
The garage-era of computing was defined by small teams building with passion and soul
Modern corporate engineering often disconnects workers from the larger vision
Open source hardware tools will democratize chip design by 2030
Trust and empowerment enable people to achieve what experts think is impossible
money_quotes: In today's age, at large companies, most engineers take jobs where they work on one teeny aspect of a machine or website or app. Sometimes they are not even aware of the larger goal or vision but just their own little world.
The team wasn't driven by power or greed, but by accomplishment and self-fulfillment. They put a part of themselves in the machine therefore producing a machine with a soul.
It's amazing what a person can do when they don't know something is impossible and are empowered to take on a task.

I think there is a different class of programmer... the idealists_dreamers? Those who build things w soul, value accomplishments & being a part of something bigger than themselves. I feel like we've lost some of that.

The end of Dennard scaling and Moore's Law and the deceleration of performance gains for standard microprocessors are not problems that must be solved but facts that, recognized, offer breathtaking opportunities.
Hardware startups will flourish for the reasons that the open source software ecosystem paired with commoditized cloud compute has unleashed software startups over the past decade.
key_insight: The end of Moore's Law combined with open source hardware tools creates an opportunity to return to building computers with soul—small teams driven by passion and accomplishment rather than corporate fragmentation.
